summary:
You are given the head of a linked list and an integer k. Reverse the nodes of the list in consecutive groups of k, and return the new head.

If the final group has fewer than k nodes remaining, leave those nodes in their original order. Only the links may be changed, not the node values.

examples.explanation: Each pair is reversed; the leftover node 5 stays as-is.
The first group of three is reversed; the remaining two nodes are too few to form a group, so they keep their order.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 5000].
1 <= k <= number of nodes in the list
Modify node links only; do not change the values stored in the nodes.
